source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/02_ADD (HAD1417MCW)/▶ CE MDD/■ 4. ADD(HAD1417MCW) Dimdi 등록관련 기술문서 제출 220215/D TCF/Attachment 07. Performance Test Report/07-01. EN 62220-1-1 Test report (A-TCF-5-058).pdf

sourceSummary.oneLine: EN 62220-1-1 test report for the ADD digital flat panel X-ray detector.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports technical documentation for performance characteristics including MTF, DQE, NPS, pixel defects, exposure conditions, readout, and regulatory/EMC report references.
keyPoints: The report states that MTF and DQE measurements were tested by EN 62220-1-1(2015).
ADD is described as a static X-ray detector fixed at 1 fps.
Active pixel area size is 345.24 mm x 425.6 mm with 2466 x 3040 active area pixels.
The scintillator is CsI and converts X-ray energy to visible light.
Reported ADD DQE values are 49.5% at 0.1 lp/mm, 43.8% at 0.5 lp/mm, 39.7% at 1.0 lp/mm, 31.6% at 2.0 lp/mm, and 21.5% at 3.0 lp/mm.

entities.name: EN 62220-1-1
entities.evidence: Test report was provided for Solid-State X-ray Imaging Devices, which includes MTF and DQE measurements as tested by EN 62220-1-1(2015)

entities.name: Piranha205
entities.evidence: The value of Ka is the absorbed dose (μGy) in free air at the image plane measured using a solid state detector dosimeter (Piranha205)

concepts.name: Detective Quantum Efficiency
concepts.evidence: The detective quantum efficiency (DQE) was evaluated as a function of spatial frequency over a wide range of incident exposure levels.

concepts.name: Modulation Transfer Function
concepts.evidence: Provide a plot of the modulation or contrast transfer function of the device.

concepts.name: Noise Power Spectrum
concepts.evidence: The NPS is the noise power spectrum measured at same air kerma.

concepts.name: Static X-ray detector
concepts.evidence: This device is fixed at 1 fps because it is a static x-ray detector.

concepts.name: Pixel defect criteria
concepts.evidence: Pixel Defect (Cluster) -Criteria : Normal within the criteria

concepts.name: Quantum noise exposure limit
concepts.evidence: The exposure at which the x-ray quantum noise equals the additive detector noise is called the quantum noise exposure limits or noise equivalent dose.

evidence.claim: The document is EN 62220-1-1 Test Report Doc. No. A-TCF-5-058, Rev. No. 1.
evidence.evidenceText: EN 62220-1-1 Test Report Doc. No. A-TCF-5-058 Rev. No. 1

evidence.claim: The report is for the ADD digital flat panel X-ray detector.
evidence.evidenceText: EN 62220-1-1 Test Report For Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector ADD
evidence.pageTarget: ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The revision history states Rev. 1 revised the product name.
The report covers MTF and DQE measurements tested by EN 62220-1-1(2015).
evidence.evidenceText: Test report was provided for Solid-State X-ray Imaging Devices, which includes MTF and DQE measurements as tested by EN 62220-1-1(2015)

evidence.claim: The listed standard is EN 62220-1-1 Edition 1.0 2015-03.
The ADD active pixel area is 345.24 mm x 425.6 mm.
evidence.evidenceText: Active pixel area size 345.24 mm x 425.6 mm

evidence.claim: The ADD has 2466 x 3040 active area pixels, totaling 7,496,640.
evidence.evidenceText: Number of active area pixel 2466 x 3040 = 7,496,640

evidence.claim: The ADD uses a CsI scintillator to convert X-rays to visible light.
evidence.evidenceText: Scintillator • CsI • Converting X-ray to Visible light to charge Phot-Diode

evidence.claim: The device is fixed at 1 fps because it is a static X-ray detector.
evidence.evidenceText: This device is fixed at 1 fps because it is a static x-ray detector.

evidence.claim: Image data are transmitted and stored as 14-bit little-endian two-dimensional arrays without a specific header.
evidence.evidenceText: All image data shall be transmitted and stored as 14-bits little-endian (Intel bytes order) two-dimensional arrays without any specific header.

evidence.claim: The ADD measured DQE values are 49.5%, 43.8%, 39.7%, 31.6%, and 21.5% at 0.1, 0.5, 1, 2, and 3 lp/mm respectively.
The ADD measured MTF values are 96.7%, 83.6%, 63.2%, 30.9%, and 14.7% at 0.1, 0.5, 1, 2, and 3 lp/mm respectively.
The ADD measured NPS values are 10.6, 8.4, 6.2, 3.5, and 1.9 at 0.1, 0.5, 1, 2, and 3 lp/mm respectively.
The active area is in full use and no under-scanning is possible.
evidence.evidenceText: The active area of this device is 345.24 mm x 425.6 mm and is in full use and no under-scanning is possible.

evidence.claim: Readout after X-ray exposure requires at least 10 seconds before the detector is ready for the next exposure.
evidence.evidenceText: Readout of the detector after X-ray exposure requires at least 10 seconds at which time the detector is ready for the next exposure.

evidence.claim: Image readout, transferring, and pre-processing requires under 5 seconds.
evidence.evidenceText: Image readout, transferring and pre-processing requires under 5 seconds.

evidence.claim: The report references safety report 397950 and EMC reports NK-20-E-0336 and NK-20-E-0337 for regulatory approvals and EMC.
evidence.evidenceText: The ADD meets the regulatory standards as outlined in the ADD safety report: 397950, EMC report: NK-20-E-0336, NK-20-E-0337

evidence.claim: The test exposure condition was RQA 5, 70 kV, 200 mA, SID 150 cm, Al 21 mm, and 40 mAs.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority or jurisdiction is not stated in the source text; authoritySignal metadata says MFDS/domestic but the report text does not identify MFDS.
Source path references HAD1417MCW and productSignal is HAD/A/F 1417-1717, but the report text identifies the product as ADD.
Some equations and figure content appear degraded in OCR, including DQE and quantum noise formulas.
The report references IEC standards in safety and EMC sections but does not list the specific IEC standard numbers there.
